mechanism_narrative: Persistently low federal funds rates reduce the discount rate for future corporate earnings, directly increasing present equity valuations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns in low-rate periods are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 7.0 percent, consumer spending power contracts sharply, reducing corporate revenue growth forecasts and driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Forward 60-day returns remain positive in over 55 percent of months where UNRATE exceeds 7.0, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous month-over-month increases in retail sales and housing starts confirm broad-based consumer demand recovery, which forces equity analysts to upgrade earnings models and produces positive S&P 500 forward returns within one month.
failure_condition: The joint positive delta condition yields negative 20-day forward returns in more than 60 percent of occurrences, or the interaction term coefficient becomes statistically insignificant in a multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month rebound in the index of industrial production signals immediate capacity utilization improvements, triggering short-term momentum buying that pushes S&P 500 returns positive over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following positive INDPRO deltas are negative in over 50 percent of cases, or the signal fails to beat a buy-and-hold benchmark over a 12-month walk-forward test.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 255.0, rising input costs compress corporate profit margins faster than pricing power can compensate, leading to negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Quarterly forward returns remain positive in over 65 percent of months where CPIAUCSL exceeds 255.0, or the negative correlation breaks down during periods of strong nominal GDP growth.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls and a decline in the unemployment rate confirms robust labor market expansion, which boosts consumer confidence and generates positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The combined positive payroll and negative unemployment delta condition yields negative 20-day returns in more than 55 percent of instances, or the interaction effect vanishes in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Higher quarterly real GDP growth directly increases corporate earnings expectations, creating immediate positive price pressure that translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next five trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns show no statistically significant positive correlation with GDPC1 levels over a 3-year rolling window, or Rank IC remains below 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: When the industrial production index falls below 98.0, manufacturing output contraction signals severe supply-side bottlenecks and weakening economic activity, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: Monthly forward returns remain positive in over 60 percent of periods where INDPRO drops below 98.0, or the signal fails to predict return direction with accuracy exceeding 52 percent in walk-forward tests.
